_comment: SynthesisPromptArgs the research-lab report-synthesis persona is grounded on. Shapes mirror src/api/researchLab/researchLab.ts (ResearchProject / ResearchHypothesis / ResearchExperiment / ResearchFinding). longFindingDescription exercises the 300-char truncation cap in buildSynthesisPrompt.

project.name: Hybrid Retrieval Latency Study
project.description: Comparing dense-only vs hybrid dense+lexical retrieval on tail queries for the org-memory service.
project.thesis: Hybrid retrieval beats dense-only on rare tail queries without hurting p95 latency.

hypotheses.statement: Adding BM25 lexical signal raises NDCG@10 on tail queries by at least 3 points.
Reciprocal-rank fusion adds under 5ms to p95 retrieval latency.

experiments.name: NDCG@10 sweep on the tail-query benchmark
experiments.methodology: Run dense-only and RRF-fused retrieval over the 500-query tail set; report NDCG@10 and leakage rate.

findings.title: Hybrid RRF lifts tail NDCG@10 from 0.79 to 0.876 with zero leaks
findings.description: On the 500-query tail benchmark, reciprocal-rank fusion of dense + BM25 improved NDCG@10 by 8.6 points over dense-only while holding the cross-user leak count at zero.

findings.title: Fusion latency overhead is within budget
findings.description: The reciprocal-rank fusion pass added measurable but bounded latency across every percentile we sampled, and the overhead stayed inside the service-level budget even under the most adversarial concurrent-load profile we could construct, which included cold-cache tail queries interleaved with large batch reindex jobs running on the same node, meaning the fused path never breached the p95 ceiling the SLA commits to downstream consumers.
